threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Overground' and not just 'the orange line.' Also, your flat's still too quiet for a Hackney street.
Last week on the N35, a woman serenaded the driver with a full Whitney Houston ballad. Even the usual drunks sat in awe.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw an old man in a bathrobe chasing a runaway dog past a kebab shop, I knew I was home in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can find spontaneous art installations popping up by the Spree, like the one at Wiesenweg, with no permits. Just pure Berlin creativity.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they can name the taqueria on 24th that took over the old laundromat, but tourists still think it's all about the burritos on Mission.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Just saw a guy on the 14 Mission swing his backpack off like it was a dance move. Perfectly timed with the bus lurching to a stop. Classic Muni.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: They think Gràcia is just about Gaudí and fancy cafés, but the real charm is in the tiny art studios tucked behind those bustling plazas.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones at the local café asking for 'un café con leche' in nearly perfect Spanish but still squinting at the menu like it's a puzzle.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You keep trying to order a pint after 10 PM and getting confused stares from the bar staff. Welcome to Hackney's late-night scene!
Caught the 37 last night, a guy loudly arguing with a seat about how they both deserve better lives. Guess the night brings out the philosophers.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a group of kids using an old mattress as a trampoline in the park, laughing as if it were the best day ever—pure Neukölln spirit.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you stumble on a rooftop cinema screening old films while the sun sets over the Spree. It's a vibe like no other.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a true local by how they order their burrito—no rice, extra salsa, and always a Coke. Tourists go for the full burrito, every time.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 14 late night on Valencia, and the driver blasted classic jazz. A mini concert while cruising past taco trucks felt like a slice of SF magic.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the squares, but the real vibe is in the narrow streets at night, where locals gather for secret concerts.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their struggle with the metro maps; they still ask locals for directions to places that are just around the corner.
